failure_modes.text: Conjunction-fallacy style optimism can make a complex plan look safer than the uncertainty really permits.
failure_modes.mitigation: Decompose the plan into steps and check conjunctive probability: if too many independent things must go right simultaneously, the compound risk is much higher than any single-step estimate suggests.

failure_modes.text: Treating uncertainty like measurable risk can convert ignorance into false precision and irreversible commitments.
failure_modes.mitigation: Label what you don't know honestly: when uncertainty is genuine, use scenario ranges and decision rules rather than converting ignorance into false-precision point estimates.

failure_modes.text: Decision paralysis from complexity can trap the team in richer scenario language without commitment rules.
failure_modes.mitigation: Set commitment rules for each scenario: pair scenario analysis with explicit 'if X then we do Y' triggers so richer uncertainty language does not become richer paralysis.

premortem_questions.text: Is the worst case bad enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough?

premortem_questions.text: What part of this plan is genuinely measurable risk, and what part is uncertainty that has not earned point estimates yet?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What part of this plan is genuinely measurable risk, and what part is uncertainty that has not earned point estimates yet?

premortem_questions.text: If this decision goes wrong because the distribution itself moved, which threshold, dependency, or commitment fails first?
premortem_questions.source_quote: If this decision goes wrong because the distribution itself moved, which threshold, dependency, or commitment in this plan fails first?

heuristics.text: Classify the situation before committing so commitment size matches certainty level.
Use scenario analysis and simulations to inspect ranges and failure envelopes instead of one base-case forecast.
Prefer reversible probes or no-regrets moves when uncertainty has not yet been converted into stable risk.
Precommit exit conditions before escalating so the team does not have to invent kill criteria while stress, sunk effort, or narrative attachment are already active.
heuristics.source_quote: big bets ... only after uncertainty has been converted into something decision-worthy

curation_notes.summary: This source is naturally chunk-ready because it already distinguishes risk classes, reversibility, and threshold-aware pre-mortem questions.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor case examples that repeated uncertainty routing patterns in scenario-specific form.
open_questions: The heuristic chunks compress a longer strategy schema, but all three remain directly grounded in the raw playbook.

allies: The raw allies section is rich on systems thinking, logic trees, hypothesis work, and margin of safety.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section gives future relation value around System 1, confirmation bias, availability bias, and curse of knowledge.
structured_tensions: The raw source explicitly preserves a structured tension against confirmation-bias that should be retained for a later relation wave.
